title: Require Password to Disable Password Protect
author: balrammishra
body_snippet:
I noticed that when a password is set to encrypt connection configurations or the application, anyone can disable it without restriction.

Could we add an option that requires entering the current password before disabling the password protect feature?

comments.author: balrammishra
comments.snippet:
> what version you are reffering?

I checked latest release as well as stable version, every version have this issue.

comments.author: janborup
comments.snippet:
Hi @Kvarkas 

Refering to PreRelease: https://github.com/mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG/releases/tag/2025.02.05-v1.78.1-nb

Right now, in this above version, I cannot change or fill out password on any connection, can this be fixed or can I **disable this Password protect feature**, to fill our a password ???

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/84f88200-c666-4a8c-b53a-68feab0b2536)

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: this is due securestring limitations, component not supported anymore in dev and I cant find a way how to fix this, so please use older version to create a connection for a while, this week I expect will be ready a reworked version what will alow that due a different way how mR is holding credentials. btw default branch has been switch to .net 9 -> 1.78.2 but still developing and testing so no compiled version yet.

comments.author: Alessio71
comments.snippet:
Any updates regarding this matter?
Thanks

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Packaged fix in PR #3121: require current password before disabling Password protect (issue #2673), plus a verification guard in the new-password dialog.  
 
PR: PR #3121 on `mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG`

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available in community release**

This fix is included in a community build from our fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). Available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and let us know if the issue is fully resolved.
